title: BlackBerry Delivers Consistency to Investors
text: BlackBerry Ltd. has experienced a remarkable stock surge following strong quarterly earnings, with shares climbing nearly 95% in six weeks—from $4.38 in late March to $8.51 by mid-May 2026. The Canadian technology company reported four consecutive quarters of positive net earnings and three consecutive quarters of improving total revenues. In the fiscal fourth quarter, revenues rose 10% year-over-year, while the QNX division—which produces embedded software for 275 million vehicles—saw 20% growth. Rather than chasing dramatic rebounds, investors are valuing BlackBerry's newfound stability. The company's QNX division provides consistent revenue through a backlog valued at $950 million. CEO John Giamatteo stated the company is now firmly focused on growth and value creation. This consistency enables traditional valuation metrics. The stock currently trades at an estimated price-to-earnings ratio of 27, making it assessable by conventional investment analysis rather than speculation alone. While projected revenues for fiscal 2027 range between $584-611 million (6-11% growth), analyst Todd Coupland's bullish forecast of US$6 suggests current prices may be running ahead of expectations. However, the turnaround appears grounded in actual earnings rather than hopes.
